message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR Map/Array canonical length-prefix fuzz target (resolves b2jvz)

to_canonical_cbor MUST emit Array (Major Type 4, head 0x80-0x9b) and
Map (Major Type 5, head 0xa0-0xbb) length prefixes in shortest-form
per RFC 8949 §4.2.1. Length-prefix table:
  count ∈ [0,23]   → 1-byte head
  count ∈ [24,255] → 0x98/0xb8 + 1-byte length
  count ∈ [256,65535] → 0x99/0xb9 + 2-byte length
  count > 65535    → 0x9a/0xba + 4 or 0x9b/0xbb + 8 bytes

Existing cbor_int_canonical_length (lwzfk) and cbor_string_canonical_length
(h4al7) cover int + string boundaries; cbor_map_canonical_relations
(r5v32) covers map ordering. NOT covered: map/array length-prefix
encoding boundaries.

Four properties driven over count-range discriminator:
- Array length boundary (head_len + count*1B for Null elements)
- Map length boundary (head_len + per-key int boundary + Null value)
- Head-byte major-type binding (Array=4, Map=5)
- Ciborium round-trip preserves count and type

Once-gated anchors verify exact head bytes at boundary counts:
- Array: 0→0x80; 23→0x97; 24→0x98 0x18; 255→0x98 0xff; 256→0x99 01 00;
  1024→0x99 04 00
- Map: 0→0xa0; 23→0xb7; 24→0xb8 0x18; 255→0xb8 0xff; 256→0xb9 01 00;
  1024→0xb9 04 00

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
